Really bright guy. And one of the

you how do you derive meaning from your
life?
And he said an answer that really really
shook me. um which doesn't happen that
much given my worldview. He said, 'Why
do you think that life needs to be
meaningful?
And it was just this really interesting
question, which is basically that I had
an unspoken demand of the universe. Life
should be meaningful. I should be happy.
And so it's the shoulds that we don't
even know that we think and say, that
are the ones that change us the most
like hardcore. And so my favorite quote

front of a few of the books that I have
and will probably be the one that'll be
on my my tombstone, is a permutation of
an orson Scott card quote, which is we
question all of our beliefs except for
those that we truly believe and those we
never think to question.
>> Mhm.
>> And so I would say that a lot of my
entrepreneurial career, even just human

that I so so inherently believe that I
don't don't even see them. I don't even

have had this inherent demand of the
universe that my life be meaningful.
And now people would hear this and be
like, they have their shoulds and

why do I do these things? I do these
